title: Multipart Upload State, Parts, Checksums, and Cleanup · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs multipart upload state, parts, checksums, and cleanup from browser, Go service, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for recoverable multipart-upload state machine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose single versus multipart transfer and part size from object size, part-count limits, memory, network, retry, and completion constraints.
Persist upload ID, part number, offset, size, checksum, ETag, attempt, and owner so completion uses the exact accepted part list.
Bound part workers and bytes, cancel safely, drain results, preserve completed evidence, and prevent goroutine or buffer leaks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Abort Lifecycle Cleanup: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go file-delivery team handles case 8934 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate multipart part state, retained ETag evidence, cancellation, and abort cleanup; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Abort known failures, inventory abandoned uploads, and configure AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload lifecycle cleanup where supported.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Abort Lifecycle Cleanup.
steps.why: Retrieving Abort Lifecycle Cleanup through the concrete recoverable multipart-upload state machine case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Abort Lifecycle Cleanup, keep Abort Lifecycle Cleanup connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Abort known failures, inventory abandoned uploads, and configure AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload lifecycle cleanup where supported.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Abort Lifecycle Cleanup, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Abort Lifecycle Cleanup precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Abort Lifecycle Cleanup, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Go file-delivery team handles case 8934 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate multipart part state, retained ETag evidence, cancellation, and abort cleanup; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Abort known failures, inventory abandoned uploads, and configure AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload lifecycle cleanup where supported.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Abort Lifecycle Cleanup, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Incomplete multipart parts expire automatically and never incur storage cost.
